data.name: onboarding.welcome.title
data.comment: Heading on the first-run onboarding page. Short noun phrase; appears as h1 in the Blazor WebView. Anchor is the desktop local-first app — keep tone friendly, not corporate.

data.name: onboarding.welcome.detail
data.value: Set up your local workspace to start collaborating offline-first.
data.comment: Body text under the welcome title. Full sentence; conveys that Anchor works offline. "Local-first" is a deliberate technical choice — translators may use the closest cultural equivalent (e.g., "device-resident", "self-hosted").

data.name: onboarding.join-team.title
data.comment: Title on the QR-onboarding page where the user scans a code from another Anchor instance to join their team. Imperative phrasing; pairs with a "Scan QR code" call-to-action.

data.name: onboarding.join-team.detail
data.value: Scan the QR code shown by another team member to share your workspace.
data.comment: Body text on the join-team page. Full sentence; describes the QR-pairing flow per ADR 0032 (multi-team Anchor workspace switching).

data.name: errors.network-unavailable
data.comment: Status message shown when Anchor cannot reach any sync peer. Important: NOT an error from the user's perspective — Anchor is local-first, so offline is a normal mode. Tone should be neutral/informational.

data.name: errors.network-unavailable.detail
data.value: Your changes are saved locally and will sync when peers are reachable.
data.comment: Body text under the offline status. Reassures the user that work is preserved. "Peers" is used over "server" because Anchor is peer-to-peer per the local-node architecture paper.

data.name: errors.team-not-found
data.comment: Error shown when the user attempts to switch to a team that no longer exists in the local team registry. Generic enough to use across switcher / join / migration flows.

data.name: errors.team-not-found.detail
data.value: This team is no longer available on this device. Re-join via QR code if needed.
data.comment: Body text on team-not-found. Suggests the recovery path (re-join via QR). Anchor v2 supports multiple teams per ADR 0032; teams can be removed via the switcher.

data.name: sysreq.title.preinstall
data.comment: H1 heading on the PreInstallFullPage system requirements check. Short noun phrase; shown before the user installs a bundle.

data.name: sysreq.verdict.pass
data.comment: Banner text when OverallVerdict is Pass. Full sentence; reassuring tone. Shown with a green visual indicator.

data.name: sysreq.verdict.warn
data.value: Your system meets minimum requirements with warnings
data.comment: Banner text when OverallVerdict is WarnOnly. Full sentence; one or more Recommended dimensions failed but no Required dimension failed. Shown with an amber visual indicator.

data.name: sysreq.verdict.block
data.value: Your system does not meet the minimum requirements
data.comment: Banner text when OverallVerdict is Block. Full sentence; at least one Required dimension failed. Shown with a red visual indicator.

data.name: sysreq.dimension.Hardware.name
data.comment: Display name for the Hardware dimension evaluation row (DimensionChangeKind.Hardware per ADR 0062). Short noun; visible as a row label in the requirements table.

data.name: sysreq.dimension.User.name
data.comment: Display name for the User dimension (DimensionChangeKind.User per ADR 0062). Covers user-role and account-tier checks.

data.name: sysreq.dimension.Regulatory.name
data.comment: Display name for the Regulatory dimension (DimensionChangeKind.Regulatory per ADR 0062). Covers jurisdiction and compliance checks.

data.name: sysreq.dimension.Runtime.name
data.comment: Display name for the Runtime dimension (DimensionChangeKind.Runtime per ADR 0062). Covers .NET/OS version checks.

data.name: sysreq.dimension.FormFactor.name
data.comment: Display name for the FormFactor dimension (DimensionChangeKind.FormFactor per ADR 0062). Covers desktop/mobile/tablet distinctions.

data.name: sysreq.dimension.Edition.name
data.comment: Display name for the Edition dimension (DimensionChangeKind.Edition per ADR 0062). Covers Sunfish edition/tier checks (e.g., Community vs Professional).

data.name: sysreq.dimension.Network.name
data.comment: Display name for the Network dimension (DimensionChangeKind.Network per ADR 0062). Covers connectivity and peer-reachability checks.

data.name: sysreq.dimension.Trust.name
data.comment: Display name for the TrustAnchor dimension (DimensionChangeKind.TrustAnchor per ADR 0062). Key uses "Trust" (user-facing shorthand); covers key-attestation and identity-root checks.

data.name: sysreq.dimension.SyncState.name
data.comment: Display name for the SyncState dimension (DimensionChangeKind.SyncState per ADR 0062). Covers local-first sync health checks.

data.name: sysreq.dimension.VersionVector.name
data.comment: Display name for the VersionVector dimension (DimensionChangeKind.VersionVector per ADR 0062). Covers CRDT version-compatibility checks.

data.name: sysreq.policy.Required
data.comment: Policy badge label for DimensionPolicyKind.Required. A Required+Fail dimension blocks install. Short adjective; shown as a badge next to the dimension name.

data.name: sysreq.policy.Recommended
data.comment: Policy badge label for DimensionPolicyKind.Recommended. A Recommended+Fail dimension produces a WarnOnly verdict. Short adjective.

data.name: sysreq.policy.Informational
data.comment: Policy badge label for DimensionPolicyKind.Informational. An Informational dimension never blocks or warns at the verdict level. Short adjective.

data.name: sysreq.policy.Unevaluated
data.comment: Policy badge label for DimensionPolicyKind.Unevaluated. Shown when a dimension was skipped or could not be assessed. User-facing; "Not checked" is friendlier than the internal enum name.

data.name: sysreq.status.pass
data.comment: Accessible aria-label for a dimension row whose outcome is Pass. Used as the status icon's accessible name when the icon (✓) is decorative. SC 1.1.1.

data.name: sysreq.status.warn
data.comment: Accessible aria-label for a dimension row whose outcome is Fail and policy is Recommended. SC 1.1.1.

data.name: sysreq.status.block
data.comment: Accessible aria-label for a dimension row whose outcome is Fail and policy is Required. SC 1.1.1. "Fails — required" conveys both failure and criticality.

data.name: sysreq.status.unevaluated
data.comment: Accessible aria-label for a dimension row whose outcome is Unevaluated. SC 1.1.1.

data.name: sysreq.action.force_install
data.comment: Button label for operator-level force-install when verdict is Block. Per ADR 0063-A1.11. The parenthetical "(operator override)" signals elevated authority. Only shown when the overall verdict is Block.

data.name: sysreq.action.install_anyway
data.comment: Button label when verdict is WarnOnly. User acknowledges warnings and proceeds. Not shown when verdict is Block — use force_install for that.

data.name: sysreq.action.continue
data.comment: Primary CTA button label when verdict is Pass. Short imperative; leads to the next install step.

data.name: sysreq.recovery.try_this
data.comment: Header label above an OperatorRecoveryAction block. Short imperative with colon; introduces the suggested recovery step for a failing dimension.

data.name: sysreq.status.checking
data.comment: Live-region text shown while the async EvaluateAsync call is in flight. Folded into the single verdict live region per B5/B6 council fix.

data.name: sysreq.error.bundle_not_found
data.value: Bundle "{0}" was not found in the catalog.
data.comment: Live-region error text when IBundleCatalog.TryGet returns false. {0} is the BundleId route parameter. Per B7: demoted from role="alert" to role="status" (not interruption-worthy). Format placeholder is positional per IStringLocalizer convention.

data.name: sysreq.action.force_install_in_progress
data.comment: Button label + sr-only live-region text while the force-install async operation is in flight. Per B4 council fix: button text changes + a polite live region announces the in-progress state to AT users.

data.name: sysreq.dim_list.label
data.comment: Accessible aria-label on the dimension list <ul>. Per B9 council fix: localized rather than hardcoded English. SC 1.3.1.

data.name: sysreq.inline.requirement_not_met
data.comment: aria-label on the fail badge inside SystemRequirementsInlinePanel summary. SC 1.1.1 non-text character (!) needs a text alternative.

data.name: sysreq.inline.detail_fail_default
data.value: This requirement is not met on your device.
data.comment: Fallback detail text when DimensionEvaluation.Detail is null and Outcome is Fail.

data.name: sysreq.inline.detail_pass_default
data.comment: Fallback detail text when DimensionEvaluation.Detail is null and Outcome is Pass.

data.name: sysreq.regression.banner_title
data.comment: Heading in the PostInstallRegressionBanner assertive region; announces the regression event.

data.name: sysreq.regression.list_label
data.comment: aria-label for the polite additions-only list of regressing Required dimensions (C4 amendment).
